name: Doppelhaushälfte Schalksmühle Weidenstr. (1956)
character: 1956 Doppelhaushälfte (semi-detached half) with cellar, gable roof, two shed dormers per long facade
source_origin: scanned paper Baubeschreibung + Ansichten/Grundrisse/DG/EG PDFs + exposé + cadastral
agent_notes: Most architecturally clean of the 3 testhouses — elongated ~2:1 footprint, clear 3-level stack, exposed cellar on Tal side. Good baseline for the convergence loop.

name: Boss SFH on hillside (2007)
character: Modern single-family house on steep hillside lot with daylight basement on the east elevation
site: hillside lot, ~3.8 m E-W slope, daylight basement exposed to east
agent_notes: Stress-tests sloped toposolid (heightSamples) and L-shape massing with attached garage. The hillside is the single most visually distinctive feature — flat-toposolid render = 2/10.

name: Historicist Doppelhaus mit Zwerchhaus + Schleppgauben
character: Historicist semi-detached house with twin cross-gables (Zwerchhaus / Zwerchgiebel), arched Zwerchgauben, Schleppgauben, carport with spiral stair, 5 stacked levels including Spitzboden
agent_notes: Most demanding of the 3 — cross-gables, ornament, 5-level stack push the engine. Currently bim-ai cannot render the cross-gable read (MF-22 dormer mesh, EA-1 half_gable), so this is the bellwether for capability-gap GH issues.
